openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker/even.purpose: Pure schema package defining RecalculateEvent — the Watermill event contract between balance recalculation producers (entitlement lifecycle hooks, ingest flush) and the balance-worker consumer. No business logic, no DB access.

patterns.name: Implement marshaler.Event with compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every event struct must implement marshaler.Event (EventName() string, EventMetadata() metadata.EventMetadata). Enforce with var _ marshaler.Event = RecalculateEvent{}.

patterns.name: Versioned EventType via metadata.EventType struct
patterns.description: Event type is declared as metadata.EventType{Subsystem, Name, Version}; the event name string is derived via metadata.GetEventName(). Bump Version on breaking schema changes — never rename the Go type instead.
patterns.example: recalculateEventType = metadata.EventType{Subsystem: EventSubsystem, Name: RecalculateEventName, Version: "v2"}

patterns.name: OperationType enum with Values() + Validate()
patterns.description: New operation kinds must be added to both the const block and the Values() slice. Validate() uses slices.Contains(o.Values(), o) — omitting an entry from Values() makes it always invalid.
patterns.example: func (o OperationType) Validate() error { if !slices.Contains(o.Values(), o) { return fmt.Errorf(...) } return nil }

patterns.name: EventMetadata.Subject via ComposeResourcePath
patterns.description: Subject must always be built with metadata.ComposeResourcePath(namespace, entityKind, id) — never raw string concatenation.

key_file_guides.role: Single source of truth for the recalculate event schema: struct fields, OperationType enum, and all marshaler.Event plumbing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields is a wire-format change — consumers must be updated simultaneously. Version string in recalculateEventType must be bumped on breaking changes. EventVersionSubsystem is exported from this file and used by eventbus routing — do not rename.

anti_patterns: Adding DB calls or business logic — this is a pure schema/data package
Constructing RecalculateEvent without calling Validate() before publishing
Hard-coding the event name string instead of using metadata.GetEventName(recalculateEventType)
Introducing a new OperationType constant without adding it to Values()
Renaming EventVersionSubsystem — it is the routing key used by eventbus.GeneratePublishTopic

decisions.decision: Event schema lives in events/ sub-package rather than inline in the worker
decisions.rationale: Producers outside the worker (entitlement service hooks, ingest flush) import this package to construct events without depending on the full worker package, avoiding circular imports.

decisions.decision: Version encoded in EventType struct ('v2'), not in the Go type name
decisions.rationale: Allows multiple consumers running different versions to coexist on the same Kafka topic during rolling deploys by filtering on EventVersionSubsystem.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new OperationType and publish a valid RecalculateEvent

openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker/filt.purpose: Pluggable filter chain that gates whether a RecalculateEvent should be processed for a given namespace/entitlement. Prevents redundant ClickHouse queries via high-watermark caching and short-circuits namespaces with no active notification rules.

patterns.name: Filter / NamedFilter / CalculationTimeRecorder interface hierarchy
patterns.description: All implementations must satisfy Filter (IsNamespaceInScope + IsEntitlementInScope). Named filters also implement Name() string. Filters that update state after calculation implement CalculationTimeRecorder. All enforce with compile-time var _ assertions.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() called first in constructor
patterns.description: Each filter with external dependencies takes a typed Config struct implementing models.Validator. The constructor calls cfg.Validate() as its first statement and returns error on failure — never panics.

patterns.name: HighWatermarkBackend abstraction for storage
patterns.description: HighWatermarkCache delegates all storage to HighWatermarkBackend interface (Get/Record). Only in-memory LRU is implemented now. New backends (Redis) must implement HighWatermarkBackend — never add storage directly to HighWatermarkCache.

patterns.name: LRU+TTL cache for all external service lookups
patterns.description: Any filter that calls an external service (notification, DB) must cache results using lrux.CacheWithItemTTL — filters run per-event on the hot worker path and must not issue unbounded external calls.

patterns.name: Reset operations always pass high-watermark filter
patterns.description: snapshot.ValueOperationReset must bypass the watermark check and return true unconditionally — fresh notification events for new periods must always flow through.
patterns.example: if req.Operation == snapshot.ValueOperationReset { return true, nil }

patterns.name: EntitlementFilterRequest.Validate() inside every IsEntitlementInScope
patterns.description: All IsEntitlementInScope implementations must call req.Validate() first and return the error. Zero-value EventAt silently bypasses watermark comparisons if not validated.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Filter, NamedFilter, CalculationTimeRecorder interfaces and the EntitlementFilterRequest / RecordLastCalculationRequest input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntitlementFilterRequest.Validate() is the canonical guard — all implementations must call it. Adding fields here is a breaking change for all filter implementations.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory LRU cache preventing recalculation for events older than the last successful calculation timestamp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultClockDrift (-1ms) is subtracted from the watermark in comparisons — replicate this offset in any new time comparison or events under clock skew will be dropped. HighWatermarkInMemoryBackend.cache is not goroutine-safe without lru's built-in locking — do not bypass it.

key_file_guides.role: Skips recalculation for namespaces/entitlements with no active BalanceThreshold notification rules. Caches rule lookups with TTL via lrux.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Empty Features list in a BalanceThreshold rule means 'all features in scope' — any new feature-matching logic must preserve this semantics. NotificationsFilter does not implement CalculationTimeRecorder (no RecordLastCalculation) — do not add watermark logic here.

anti_patterns: Calling external services inside IsEntitlementInScope without LRU+TTL caching — runs per-event and will saturate downstream services
Implementing a new filter without a compile-time var _ interface assertion
Skipping cfg.Validate() in a new filter constructor — nil dependencies cause nil-pointer panics at runtime rather than startup
Adding mutable state to filter structs without concurrent-access protection — filters are called concurrently from worker goroutines
Hardcoding storage into HighWatermarkCache instead of implementing a new HighWatermarkBackend

decisions.decision: Filters composed as a named chain rather than a single monolithic gating function
decisions.rationale: Each filter has a distinct concern (watermark vs notification rule existence) with different caching strategies. The chain allows the worker to short-circuit on the cheapest filter (namespace-level) before the more expensive entitlement-level check.

decisions.decision: HighWatermarkCache uses a pluggable HighWatermarkBackend interface despite only in-memory being implemented
decisions.rationale: Future Redis-backed persistence would allow high-watermark state to survive worker restarts, eliminating post-crash redundant ClickHouse queries across all entitlements.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new NamedFilter that skips entitlements whose feature is archived
